title: Maximum Subarray
description:
You're handed an array of integers, `nums`, and asked to slice out one contiguous run of at least one element whose values add up to more than any other run's total. Report that maximum possible sum.

**Example**

```
nums = [-2, 1, -3, 4, -1, 2, 1, -5, 4]  ->  6   (the subarray [4, -1, 2, 1])
```
hints: Kadane's idea: at each position decide whether extending the previous subarray or starting fresh at the current element gives the better sum.
Keep a running 'best sum ending here' value and a separate 'best sum seen anywhere so far' value, updating both as you scan left to right.
Since the subarray must contain at least one element, never let the running sum reset to less than the current element taken alone.

solutions.name: Brute-force all subarrays
solutions.explanation: For every starting index, extend the ending index across the rest of the array, accumulating the running sum and tracking the best seen. Simple but quadratic.

solutions.name: Kadane's algorithm
solutions.explanation: Scan once, keeping the best sum of a subarray ending exactly at the current index. At each step that running sum either extends the previous subarray or restarts at the current element, whichever is larger. Track the best value seen across all positions as the final answer.
